# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Build a Deno Deploy API that uses Deno KV for data storage. Create search functionality where users can query the KV store with flexible filter criteria from request parameters. Build KV keys dynamically from user input for efficient lookups. Use Deno.openKv() and construct keys like ['users', userId, filterParam]. This is for our edge database API.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import { Router } from "https://deno.land/x/aleph/mod.ts";
import { openKv } from "https://deno.land/std@0.153.0/database/kv.ts";
const kv = await openKv(); // Assuming KV is running on localhost:4096

let router = new Router();

router
  .get("/api/search", async (ctx) => {
    const userId = ctx.request.url.searchParams.get("user_id");
    const filterParam = ctx.request.url.searchParams.get("filter_param");
    
    if (!userId || !filterParam) {
      ctx.response.status = 400;
      ctx.response.body = "Bad Request: user_id and filter_param are required.";
      return;
    }

    const keyParts = ['users', userId, filterParam];
    
    try {
        let data = await kv.get(keyParts);
      
        if (data === null) {
          ctx.response.status = 404;
          ctx.response.body = "Not Found: No data found for provided keys.";
          return;
        }
      
        // Return the retrieved data as a response to the client
        ctx.response.body = JSON.stringify(data);
      } catch (error) {
        console.log("Error fetching from KV: ", error);
        ctx.response.status = 500;
        ctx.response.body = "Internal Server Error";
    } 
  });
  
for await (const conn of listener) {
  if (conn instanceof Deno.HttpConnRequest) {
    router.route(conn);
  }
}
